title: ICE Defies Two Federal Court Orders, Makes Courthouse Arrests in New York City
excerpt: ICE agents arrested three immigrants at Manhattan immigration courts in one week, in apparent defiance of orders from two separate federal judges barring such arrests — the first reported violations of the nationwide and New York courthouse arrest bans.
content:
Beginning on June 26, 2026, ICE agents made three arrests at Manhattan immigration courts within a single week, in what immigration attorneys described as the first serious violations of orders by two separate federal judges prohibiting courthouse arrests.

  • On June 26, agents arrested an Ecuadorian man at the 26 Federal Plaza immigration court and a man from the Dominican Republic at the 290 Broadway court — both in Lower Manhattan — in apparent violation of SDNY Judge Kevin Castel's May 18 order.
  • On June 29, agents detained a Guatemalan man at 290 Broadway, the third arrest in a week at Manhattan immigration courts.
  • Advocates from Make the Road New York filed legal challenges arguing the arrests violated not only the right to due process but also brazenly flouted the federal court order.
  • A spokesperson for ICE denied any violation of a court order but did not explain how the arrests fit within the narrow exceptions permitted under Judge Castel's ruling.

The arrests appeared to follow a pattern of non-compliance with judicial orders previously documented in Colorado, Rhode Island, and other jurisdictions. Attorneys described a 'growing pattern of increased ICE detentions' in direct contradiction of court rulings requiring agents to operate within specific legal authority — a pattern traced in part to inadequate training on court orders and arrest authority.
mediaPlaceholder: Video footage and court documents from the New York courthouse showing ICE arrests in defiance of court orders
imageAlt: ICE Defies Two Federal Court Orders, Makes Courthouse Arrests in New York City
